Title: MONOPTEROS isoform MP11ir plays a role during somatic embryogenesis in Arabidopsis thaliana

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: aminotransferase involved in IPyA auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone controlling embryogenic transition and somatic embryogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: IZE explants during somatic embryogenesis; hypocotyl/cotyledon regions with auxin response
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: TAA/TAR entry step of IPyA auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts tryptophan toward the IPyA auxin precursor pool in the TAA/TAR-YUC pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher TAA1 expression/localization is associated with increased endogenous auxin and altered somatic embryogenesis; reduced MP function lowers TAA1 expression and impairs SE
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in ΔARF5 overexpression, bdl induction, mp mutant, and complementation lines; GUS translational reporter localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly active throughout IZE explants during embryogenic transition; slight increase upon ΔARF5 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2,4-D-induced SE; TSA-induced SE; β-estradiol-induced ΔARF5 overexpression; dexamethasone-induced bdl expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TAA1 expression increased after ΔARF5 overexpression. 2. TAA1 was strongly down-regulated when MP function was reduced by bdl or mp mutation. 3. MP11ir restored TAA1 expression toward wild-type levels in complemented mpS319 explants, supporting MP/MP11ir-dependent auxin biosynthesis during SE.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DAP-seq identified MP binding peaks on the TAA1 promoter, supporting possible direct regulation by MP.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: proTAA1:TAA1-GUS (native/translational reporter); proXVE inducible for ΔARF5 context; 35S inducible for bdl context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overaccumulation of auxin biosynthesis under ΔARF5 context correlates with callus formation instead of embryos
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating TAA1 together with MP/MP11ir could tune endogenous auxin for regeneration, but excessive activation risks callus formation and reduced embryogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq profiles from Plant Cistrome for MP/ARF2 were analyzed; Zenodo repository for raw images/source data

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: aminotransferase involved in IPyA auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone controlling embryogenic transition and somatic embryogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: TAA/TAR entry step of IPyA auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts tryptophan toward the IPyA auxin precursor pool in the TAA/TAR-YUC pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TAR1 transcript is positively associated with MP/ΔARF5 activity, but spatial localization was largely unchanged
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in ΔARF5 overexpression, bdl induction, mp mutant, and complementation lines; GUS translational reporter localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Localization largely unchanged upon ΔARF5 overexpression, bdl induction, or in mp mutant
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2,4-D-induced SE; β-estradiol-induced ΔARF5 overexpression; dexamethasone-induced bdl expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TAR1 expression increased early after ΔARF5 overexpression. 2. TAR1 was down-regulated under reduced MP function. 3. Complementation showed TAR1 remained lower than Col-0 with MP11ir alone at day 10, suggesting limited rescue relative to other targets.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DAP-seq identified MP binding peaks on the TAR1 promoter, supporting possible direct regulation by MP.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: proTAR1:TAR1-GUS (native/translational reporter); proXVE inducible for ΔARF5 context; 35S inducible for bdl context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Misregulation of TAR1 as part of broader auxin overproduction may contribute to callus formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for regeneration engineering, but effect appears weaker/more context-dependent than YUC genes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq profiles from Plant Cistrome for MP/ARF2 were analyzed; Zenodo repository for raw images/source data

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone controlling embryogenic transition and somatic embryogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: hypocotyl region and cotyledons of IZE explants during SE
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: YUC oxidation step of IPyA auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes the YUCCA-dependent conversion of IPyA-pathway precursor toward IAA production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control supported for SE-associated auxin production, but not formally designated rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: YUC3 is positively regulated by MP/MP11ir/ΔARF5 and contributes to auxin accumulation; its loss/reduction correlates with impaired SE
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in ΔARF5 overexpression, bdl induction, mp mutant, and complementation lines; YUC3-GUS localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitous in hypocotyl region; enhanced in cotyledons upon ΔARF5 overexpression; reduced by bdl; absent/reduced in mp depending on stage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2,4-D-induced SE; β-estradiol-induced ΔARF5 overexpression; dexamethasone-induced bdl expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUC3 was upregulated by ΔARF5 at day 5 but downregulated after prolonged overexpression at day 10. 2. Reduced MP function strongly decreased YUC3 expression. 3. Complementation indicated MP11ir restored YUC3 to wild-type level and combined MP+MP11ir showed synergistic effects.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper highlights YUC3 as one of the likely common and specific transcriptional targets of MP/MP11ir in SE.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: proYUC3:YUC3-GUS (native/translational reporter); proXVE inducible for ΔARF5 context; 35S inducible for bdl context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess YUC3 activity under ΔARF5 contributes to auxin overproduction and callus formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for tuning regeneration competence, especially together with MP/MP11ir, but dosage must be controlled to avoid overproduction of auxin
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Zenodo repository for raw images/source data

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone controlling embryogenic transition and somatic embryogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: YUC oxidation step of IPyA auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes the YUCCA-dependent conversion of IPyA-pathway precursor toward IAA production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control supported for SE-associated auxin production, but not formally designated rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: YUC5 is positively regulated by MP/ΔARF5 and contributes to altered auxin homeostasis affecting SE outcome
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in ΔARF5 overexpression, bdl induction, mp mutant, and complementation lines; YUC5-GUS localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Specific accumulation at cotyledon tips on day 10 under ΔARF5 overexpression; restricted to single cells in mp mutant
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2,4-D-induced SE; β-estradiol-induced ΔARF5 overexpression; dexamethasone-induced bdl expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUC5 was induced rapidly and persistently by ΔARF5 overexpression. 2. Reduced MP function decreased YUC5 expression. 3. MP11ir restored YUC5 expression toward wild-type levels in complemented lines.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DAP-seq identified MP binding peaks on the YUC5 promoter, supporting possible direct regulation by MP.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: proYUC5:YUC5-GUS (native/translational reporter); proXVE inducible for ΔARF5 context; 35S inducible for bdl context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overactivation likely contributes to excess endogenous auxin and callus instead of embryos
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for regeneration engineering but should be carefully dosage-controlled or combined with feedback management
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq profiles from Plant Cistrome for MP/ARF2 were analyzed; Zenodo repository for raw images/source data

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone controlling embryogenic transition and somatic embryogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: proximal regions of cotyledons and hypocotyl during SE
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: YUC oxidation step of IPyA auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes the YUCCA-dependent conversion of IPyA-pathway precursor toward IAA production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control supported for SE-associated auxin production, but not formally designated rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: YUC8 is strongly MP/MP11ir-dependent; its activation associates with increased auxin and callus formation under ΔARF5, while loss of MP abolishes YUC8 signal
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in ΔARF5 overexpression, bdl induction, mp mutant, and complementation lines; YUC8-GUS localization; auxin/IAA quantification under ΔARF5 context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Robust accumulation in proximal cotyledons and hypocotyl upon ΔARF5 overexpression; decreased by bdl; absent in mp mutant
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2,4-D-induced SE; β-estradiol-induced ΔARF5 overexpression; dexamethasone-induced bdl expression; TSA for auxin response imaging
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUC8 was induced by ΔARF5 and strongly reduced by bdl or mp mutation. 2. YUC8 localization is highly responsive to MP-dependent transcriptional status. 3. Complementation suggested MP has a prominent role in YUC8 regulation, with synergistic MP+MP11ir effects also observed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ARF2, not MP, showed DAP-seq peak on the YUC8 promoter in the paper's promoter analysis; regulation by MP may therefore be indirect or via distinct ARF complexes.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: proYUC8:YUC8-GUS (native/translational reporter); proXVE inducible for ΔARF5 context; 35S inducible for bdl context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong YUC8 activation contributes to excess IAA accumulation and callus formation, reducing embryogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to manipulate regeneration/embryogenic competence, but only with strict control of auxin levels
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq profiles from Plant Cistrome for MP/ARF2 were analyzed; Zenodo repository for raw images/source data

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ARF transcription factor with DNA-binding domain, middle region, and PB1 domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone controlling embryogenic transition and somatic embryogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: IZE explants during SE; local maxima in cotyledons, hypocotyl, SAM, root tip
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: central regulator required for effective embryogenic transition and for maintaining auxin biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MP promotes auxin biosynthetic gene expression and somatic embryogenesis; loss or inhibition of MP reduces SE and auxin biosynthesis gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis (mpS319), complementation with proMP:MP, DEX-inducible bdl repression, RT-qPCR, GFP localization, GUS reporter localization
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation or positive regulation of auxin biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positively regulates expression of TAA1, TAR1, YUC3, YUC5, and YUC8 during SE
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: exogenous auxins (2,4-D, NAA, IAA); TSA-induced embryogenic transition; Aux/IAA-mediated repression by BDL
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly upregulated during auxin- and TSA-induced SE; nuclear signal in SAM, cotyledons, and root tip that intensifies with culture age
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Auxin-response transcriptional complex with Aux/IAA proteins via PB1 domain
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: TSA-dependent SE induction correlates with elevated MP transcript, but direct epigenetic regulation of MP was not mechanistically established
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2,4-D, NAA, IAA, TSA, dexamethasone-inducible bdl expression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced MP activity abolishes or strongly impairs SE; mis-tuning of MP activity alters auxin homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: proMP for complementation/localization; 35S for BDL/bdl perturbation context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MP transcript accumulates strongly during auxin- and TSA-induced SE. 2. MP is required for SE, as mpS319 and inducible bdl repression impair embryogenesis. 3. MP positively regulates auxin biosynthesis genes TAA1, TAR1, YUC3, YUC5, and YUC8, linking auxin signaling to auxin production during SE.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Canonical MP alone did not fully rescue mpS319 SE defects; full rescue required both MP and MP11ir, indicating nonredundant functions.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: MP is a high-value regeneration regulator, but engineering should preserve balanced auxin homeostasis and may require isoform-aware manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq profiles for MP analyzed from Plant Cistrome; Zenodo repository for raw images/source data

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: alternatively spliced auxin response factor isoform
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: truncated ARF5/MP isoform lacking PB1 domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone controlling embryogenic transition and somatic embryogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: isoform-specific regulator required together with canonical MP for full rescue of SE and for proper control of auxin biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MP11ir supports SE and positively influences auxin biosynthesis gene expression; altered MP11ir/ΔARF5 activity changes auxin homeostasis and embryo-vs-callus fate
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression profiling, complementation with proMP:MP11ir in mpS319, ΔARF5 inducible overexpression as PB1-lacking mimic, RT-qPCR, GUS reporter localization, IAA quantification
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation, likely auxin-insensitive due to loss of PB1-mediated Aux/IAA repression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TAA1; YUC3; YUC5; possibly TAR1 and YUC8 depending on context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes expression of TAA1, YUC3, YUC5 and contributes with MP to YUC8 regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: exogenous auxins (especially 2,4-D and NAA); TSA-induced embryogenic transition
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript highly enriched during auxin- and TSA-induced SE; protein signal faint/diffuse and not clearly nuclear in available GFP lines
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: faint/diffuse; no clear nuclear localization detected in this study
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Likely reduced/absent interaction with Aux/IAA proteins due to missing PB1 domain
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: PB1 loss suggests altered inability to form canonical ARF–Aux/IAA interaction complexes
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: TSA treatment strongly increases MP11ir transcript during auxin-independent SE, but direct epigenetic mechanism was not established
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2,4-D, NAA, IAA, TSA, β-estradiol-induced ΔARF5 overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ectopic overexpression of PB1-lacking ΔARF5 mimic inhibits SE and causes callus formation via auxin overaccumulation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: proMP for complementation/localization; proXVE for ΔARF5 mimic overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MP11ir transcript is specifically and strongly induced during embryogenic transition, including TSA-induced auxin-independent SE. 2. MP11ir alone partially rescues mpS319, but full rescue requires both MP11ir and canonical MP. 3. MP11ir likely promotes auxin biosynthesis genes and, when mimicked by ΔARF5 overexpression, elevates IAA and drives callus formation instead of embryos.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MP11ir is an alternatively spliced transcript encoding a truncated protein lacking the PB1 domain, rendering it insensitive to Aux/IAA repression in the proposed model.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Isoform-aware manipulation of MP may improve regeneration systems, but PB1-lacking overexpression should be avoided or tightly controlled because it disrupts auxin homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Zenodo repository for raw images/source data
